hard.next_step: 5/4/26 Determine 26 priorities - if S2P initiative is part of focus. Invitation to global AI summit 5/13/26
hard.ais_why: AI importance logged as 'Don't Know'; ANA is vendor-tagged, not buyer-requested. On the 17 Oct 2025 discovery call neither AI, Merlin, GenAI nor autonomous negotiation came up from the buyer side; the priorities were multi-ERP integration, supplier inventory visibility, indirect spend governance and implementation cost. Wait-and-see AI posture, not hostile, but nothing yet lifts it above the lower band.

north_star_verdict.headline: S2P RFP has gone dark for 8-plus months since the only buyer call (17 Oct 2025). The rep's 04 May 2026 re-engagement email is unanswered nine weeks on; no economic buyer, no demo, no shortlist standing. Inertia, not a rival, is the biggest threat to the 23 Oct 2026 close.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 06 Oct 2025, into Formal Evaluation 15 Oct 2025 (~9 days). Time in Formal Evaluation: ~264 days (15 Oct 2025 to today, 06 Jul 2026). Close 23 Oct 2026 is 109 days out. Last buyer engagement: 17 Oct 2025 (~8.7 months ago). Last Salesforce activity 03 May 2026 (rep outreach, 64 days ago), no buyer reply. Cold from the buyer for well beyond 60 days with no buyer-facing deliverable executed, which forces Off Track under the North Star rules regardless of stage. A live RFP at 109 days out would need shortlist confirmation, demos, commercials and EB/DM mapping inside 30-60 days to stay on pace; none is in motion.
evidence: Amount: $350k, up from $300k on 15 Oct 2025 (SF field history)
Close: 23 Oct 2026, 109 days out; pushed from 20 Mar 2026 on 10 Dec 2025, a 7-month slip (SF field history)
Created 06 Oct 2025; LastActivityDate 03 May 2026 (64 days ago); last buyer call 17 Oct 2025 (~8.7 months ago)
Next Step (SF, dated 04 May 2026): Clari email to determine 2026 priorities and invite to a 13 May 2026 global AI summit; no buyer reply recorded

One Q&A call 17 Oct 2025: $120-130M addressable indirect spend, 50-60% under governance, essentially no S2P system today, SAP ECC to S/4 HANA pilot targeted end 2026/Q1 2027, RFP timeline 'too aspirational' and to be flexed (Nikhil Sahasrabuddhe, 17 Oct 2025)
Nikhil: 'I don't really feel we need to rush... we should have some flexibility... Arun should be able to come back to you next week' (17 Oct 2025) - that promised follow-up never arrived
Prior engagement pulse: COOLING (server-computed). Buyer silence has since extended past the rep's own re-engagement attempt with no reply.

metrics.narrative: Nikhil Sahasrabuddhe quantified the problem on 17 Oct 2025: $120-130M addressable indirect spend with only 50-60% under governance, leaving roughly $50-65M of unmanaged or maverick spend, plus goals of 'complete control on the overall indirect spend,' analytics they 'completely lack today,' and capturing 'pilferages.' Direct spend is a separate, larger track under Kevin Marcell but was left unquantified. No savings or ROI target has been set, and the buyer is building the value case internally with KPMG. The move is to co-build a quantified savings model against that $50-65M unmanaged spend once the buyer re-engages.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer or budget owner has ever appeared on a call or in Salesforce. Nikhil (indirect lead) drove the RFP and said he had 'conversations with the relevant people within the organization' about relaxing the timeline, implying he escalates upward but does not hold the purse. Scott Bichan (VP Lifecycle Quality Operations) joined the 17 Oct 2025 call briefly but is tangential to procurement and finance. At $350k against $120-130M addressable spend a CFO or CPO sign-off is required, and identifying then reaching that person is the top open gate for this early-stage deal.

decision_criteria.narrative: The RFP is 'fairly extensive' (Markus Vogel, 17 Oct 2025) and the criteria voiced on the call are: integration with a three-ERP landscape (SAP ECC now, Dynamics 365, future S/4 HANA), supplier collaboration and inventory visibility for 2,000+ suppliers (top 10-50 direct), contract governance and compliance at PO time via the Agiloft CLM, modular licensing transparency ('what are you licensing by module'), and an implementation cost estimate. These are confirmed asks, but the formal scoring and weighting are not visible to us. The move is to obtain the written RFP evaluation criteria and their weighting.
sources: Avoma Q&A call 17 Oct 2025 (Markus Vogel, Nikhil Sahasrabuddhe, Monazrual Haque)

decision_process.narrative: The single 17 Oct 2025 Q&A call ran under NDA with indirect (Nikhil), digital transformation (Markus Vogel), operations (Arun Pandey) and procurement (Monazrual Haque), plus a late cameo from VP Quality (Scott Bichan); the direct-sourcing lead Kevin Marcell was named as essential but was absent. The platform serves two tracks: direct procurement under Kevin and indirect under Nikhil. Nikhil then unilaterally relaxed the 'too aspirational' RFP timeline and promised Arun would confirm flexed dates 'next week,' which never happened. The process has since stalled, so the real gate is re-confirming this is a funded 2026 priority and rebuilding the milestone plan.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have surfaced, which is normal this early. Implementation would be a three-party project (Zycus plus a partner plus Revvity); KPMG, which runs Revvity's S/4 program, offered to implement Zycus but the buyer explicitly refused to lock an implementation partner early ('I wouldn't want to lock us in,' Markus Vogel, 17 Oct 2025). No MSA, SOW, legal, InfoSec, or procurement-channel discussion has begun. Visibility will only come after a shortlist decision and a commercials kickoff.
sources: Avoma Q&A call 17 Oct 2025 (Markus Vogel, John Modesto)

identify_pain.narrative: Revvity's indirect procurement is, in Nikhil's words, 'the worst in terms of automation... there's nothing really called a source-to-pay system'; they do not create POs, spend is managed 'in a very, very chaotic manner,' and they lack analytics and pilferage control (17 Oct 2025). Monazrual Haque added that supplier collaboration and forecast sharing are manual and fragmented across channels, and wants everything 'at one place.' The pain owner is Nikhil on the indirect side, with Kevin Marcell owning the unengaged direct side. The pain is real and confirmed; the missing ingredient is urgency (Nikhil: 'we don't need to rush').
sources: Avoma Q&A call 17 Oct 2025 (Nikhil Sahasrabuddhe, Monazrual Haque)

champion.narrative: Nikhil Sahasrabuddhe (indirect procurement lead, named in AIS_Why__c and on the 17 Oct 2025 call) is the closest to a champion: he led the call, articulated the pain and KPIs, and has prior positive Zycus experience ('I implemented Zycus in an early org, fairly proud of it'). But he has been silent for 8-plus months, explicitly downplayed urgency, holds no executive or budget authority, and has shown no advocacy (no EB introduction, no shared internal docs, no competitive defense). Markus Vogel is an engaged technical influencer focused on the KPMG-led S/4 program, not an S2P champion. The champion is weak and cooling, so rebuilding it ranks ahead of any commercial step.

competition.narrative: On 17 Oct 2025 Nikhil named Coupa and SAP Ariba as S2P category peers ('the kind of intelligence that tools like yours and Coupa and Arivas provide'), with no stated preference; the Salesforce competitor field is empty and no active bake-off is confirmed. SAP Ariba is the natural-fit risk given Revvity's SAP ECC footprint and S/4 journey, and Zycus's wedge is being one of only two S2P vendors with certified S/4 HANA adapters plus the 'integrate with ECC now, update to S/4 later' advice (Srishti and John Modesto, 17 Oct 2025). The genuine current threat, however, is status-quo inertia and no-decision rather than a named rival, given no S2P system today, low urgency, and 8 months of silence.

deal_movement.summary: Momentum has flatlined. The last genuine buyer engagement was the 17 Oct 2025 Q&A call; since then the close date slipped 7 months and the only touch is a 04 May 2026 rep email that the buyer has not answered. Arun Pandey's promised timeline follow-up from that call never arrived, which is the buried, still-unresolved thread.

items.change: Stage moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation; Amount raised $300k to $350k
Only buyer call to date: 17 Oct 2025 Q&A under NDA; buyer disclosed spend, ERP landscape and relaxed the RFP timeline
Close date pushed from 20 Mar 2026 to 23 Oct 2026 (7-month slip)
Rep re-engagement email (Clari): 2026 priorities and 13 May 2026 AI summit invite; no buyer reply

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest CURRENT threat on this deal is status-quo inertia and no-decision, not a rival: Revvity has essentially no S2P system today, Nikhil said 'we don't need to rush,' and the buyer has been silent for 8-plus months (17 Oct 2025 to now). Two category peers were named once, on 17 Oct 2025 by Nikhil: SAP Ariba (higher-weighted watch, the natural-fit risk given Revvity's SAP ECC footprint and live S/4 HANA journey) and Coupa (secondary, brand-level mention), neither confirmed as an active shortlist competitor and neither re-mentioned this run. Zycus holds a real wedge on both: being one of only two S2P vendors with certified S/4 HANA adapters, the pragmatic 'integrate with ECC now, update to S/4 later' path, and Nikhil's prior positive Zycus experience. No new competitor surfaced this run; the priority is not out-positioning a rival but breaking the silence to confirm the RFP is still funded and a live evaluation.

competitors.name: Status quo / no decision
competitors.quote: Nikhil (17 Oct 2025): 'I don't really feel we need to rush... we should have some flexibility... it's too aspirational' - paired with 8 months of buyer silence and no S2P system in place today.
competitors.source: Avoma Q&A call 17 Oct 2025 + engagement history
competitors.how_we_win: Attack inertia with a compelling event and a quantified cost of delay: tie the $50-65M unmanaged indirect spend and the ECC-to-S/4 integration window to a phased, low-risk first go-live so doing nothing has a visible price. Re-engage Nikhil and Arun to confirm S2P is a funded 2026 priority and reset a realistic timeline; the deal is lost to no-decision far more likely than to a rival.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Nikhil (17 Oct 2025): 'the kind of intelligence that tools like yours and Coupa and Arivas provide in terms of market intelligence... it's a huge list' (named as an S2P category peer alongside Zycus and Coupa, no explicit preference stated).
competitors.how_we_win: Pragmatic ERP integration flexibility wedge. Position Zycus as one of only two S2P players with certified S/4 HANA adapters that integrates with ECC and Dynamics 365 now and updates to S/4 later when Revvity's pilot completes (end 2026/Q1 2027), avoiding an ERP cutover that 'almost never hits the deadline' (John Modesto, 17 Oct 2025). Contrast Ariba's tightly-coupled SAP path that could force Revvity to delay S2P value on the $50-65M unmanaged spend, and make the multi-ERP integration story a demo centerpiece with Markus Vogel.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Nikhil (17 Oct 2025): 'the kind of intelligence that tools like yours and Coupa and Arivas provide in terms of market intelligence... it's a huge list' (named as an S2P category peer alongside Zycus and Ariba, no explicit preference stated).
competitors.how_we_win: Supplier-collaboration scale and contract-compliance integration wedge. Revvity has 2,000+ addressable suppliers, needs inventory visibility for the top 10-50 direct suppliers, and wants Agiloft CLM integration to enforce contract compliance at PO time. Position the Zycus supplier portal (forecast sharing, inventory upload/API, PO and catalog management on one portal) plus day-1 Agiloft integration (Srishti confirmed feasibility, 17 Oct 2025), and leverage Nikhil's prior positive Zycus implementation experience versus a newer Coupa relationship.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit is strong on integration and supplier collaboration, exposed on implementation cost and, above all, on urgency. The buyer's requirements match Zycus strengths, but nothing has been demoed or priced and the buyer has gone quiet.

best_practice_check.summary: The deal is early-stage, single-threaded, dark for 8 months, and missing an economic buyer. The win depends on breaking the silence, reaching power, and widening the thread before any competitive or commercial motion.
flags: Break the 8-month silence: owner re-engages Nikhil and Arun to confirm S2P is a funded 2026 priority - this is a no-decision/inertia risk, not a loss to a rival.
Map and reach an economic buyer: no CFO/CPO or budget owner has ever appeared; at $350k against $120-130M addressable spend, identify who funds this before investing further.
Multi-thread to the direct side: Kevin Marcell (Senior Director, Strategic Sourcing) and half the scope have never engaged; single-threading through a cooling indirect lead is fragile.

items.text: The only real engagement, the 17 Oct 2025 Q&A call, ended with the indirect lead relaxing the 'too aspirational' timeline and a promised next-week follow-up that never came, and the buyer has been silent since.
The buyer is building the business case internally with KPMG and declined Zycus's help, and no budget owner has surfaced, so we cannot yet justify or shape the price.
Coupa and SAP Ariba were named once as category peers on 17 Oct 2025 with no active bake-off confirmed; Ariba is the natural-fit watch given Revvity's SAP ECC and S/4 journey.

champion_strength.summary: Weak and cooling. Nikhil Sahasrabuddhe (indirect procurement lead) is the closest to a champion: he led the 17 Oct 2025 Q&A call, articulated the pain and KPIs clearly, and has prior positive Zycus experience ('I implemented Zycus in an early org, fairly proud of it'). But his engagement has been absent for 8-plus months, he explicitly downplayed urgency ('we don't need to rush'), he holds no executive or budget authority, and there is no evidence of advocacy (no EB introduction, no shared internal docs, no competitive defense). Rep outreach on 04 May 2026 received no reply. Markus Vogel is an engaged technical influencer but is focused on the KPMG-led S/4 program, not an S2P champion. No executive sponsor has surfaced.
champion_strength.source: Avoma Q&A call 17 Oct 2025 + SF AIS_Why__c + engagement history (no reply to 04 May 2026 outreach)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Low. ANA/Merlin is in the product scope but is vendor-tagged, not buyer-requested; the buyer never raised AI, Merlin or GenAI on the 17 Oct 2025 call and AIS is Negative (3.0). This is not an under-positioning problem to fix by pushing the Merlin story harder, because the buyer has shown no AI appetite; leading with an autonomous-negotiation narrative now would misread the room. The realistic AI angle is embedded practical value (spend analytics, supplier intelligence) rather than a standalone AI pitch.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is Negative (score 3.0): AI importance logged as 'Don't Know' and on the 17 Oct 2025 call neither AI, Merlin nor GenAI came up from the buyer; priorities were multi-ERP integration, supplier visibility and indirect spend governance. Wait-and-see, not hostile, but no AI mandate exists to lean on.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: An early-stage $350k S2P RFP that has gone dark for 8-plus months since the only real call; no economic buyer, a cooling indirect-procurement lead as our sole thread, and inertia (not a rival) as the biggest threat to the 23 Oct 2026 close.

win_position.text: We are one named vendor in an early S2P RFP that has stalled: the buyer went silent after the 17 Oct 2025 Q&A call, so there is no confirmed shortlist standing to claim.
No economic buyer or budget owner has ever surfaced; at $350k against $120-130M addressable indirect spend, nobody with sign-off authority is engaged (Avoma, 17 Oct 2025).

Our only thread, indirect lead Nikhil Sahasrabuddhe, said the timeline is 'too aspirational' and 'we don't need to rush,' and Arun Pandey's promised next-week timeline follow-up never arrived (Avoma, 17 Oct 2025).

The bright spot is fit: Nikhil implemented Zycus at a prior employer, and Zycus is one of only two S2P vendors with certified S/4 HANA adapters, matching Revvity's ECC-to-S/4 journey (Srishti and John Modesto, 17 Oct 2025).

deal_momentum.text: Momentum has flatlined: no buyer-facing event since the 17 Oct 2025 call, and the rep's 04 May 2026 re-engagement email went unanswered for over two months.
The close date already slipped seven months (20 Mar 2026 to 23 Oct 2026 on 10 Dec 2025) and the buyer deliberately relaxed the RFP timeline.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is thin: the buyer is building the business case internally with KPMG and declined Zycus's help, keeping us out of the value-justification loop (Markus Vogel, 17 Oct 2025).
No demo, POC, pricing or workshop has been scheduled; the buyer only asked for information and named Coupa and Ariba as category peers (Nikhil Sahasrabuddhe, 17 Oct 2025).

deal_risk.text: Primary risk is inertia, not a competitor: Revvity has essentially no S2P system today, low urgency, and 8 months of silence, making no-decision the likeliest outcome.
Single-threaded through a cooling indirect lead; the direct-sourcing owner Kevin Marcell has never engaged, leaving half the scope and any real power unmapped.
